pi_items.source: Task description (line 5 of task.json), setup_task.sh (line 17), export_result.sh (line 84) all consistently reference this exact path

Task description (line 5 of task.json) specifies 'Create a Table-Valued Function `Sales.tvf_GetSalesPersonMaxGap`'; setup_task.sh (lines 13-14) drops this function; export_result.sh (line 11) verifies its existence; verifier.py (line 44) validates it

Task description (line 5 of task.json) specifies 'Create a View `Sales.vw_2013_InactivityReport`'; setup_task.sh (line 12) drops this view; export_result.sh (line 12) verifies its existence; verifier.py (line 59) validates it
export_result.sh (line 56) calls the function with date range '2028-01-01' to '2028-12-31'; test data inserted with dates in 2028 (lines 27, 45)
export_result.sh: Line 27 inserts first test order with '2028-01-01', line 45 inserts second test order with '2028-01-10'; these are the two dates used for gap calculation verification

export_result.sh line 39 comment states '(Gap should be Jan 2..9 = 8 days)'; gap calculation: days between 2028-01-01 and 2028-01-10 excluding endpoints = Jan 2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9 = 8 days; export_result.sh line 75 expects output containing '8,2028-01-01,2028-01-10'

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official Microsoft AdventureWorks2022 sample database, a real public dataset downloaded from https://github.com/Microsoft/sql-server-samples/releases/download/adventureworks/AdventureWorks2022.bak. The task requires creating a Table-Valued Function named Sales.tvf_GetSalesPersonMaxGap and a View named Sales.vw_2013_InactivityReport to analyze sales data gaps. Testing logic inserts orders on 2028-01-01 and 2028-01-10, expecting a calculated gap of 8 days (Jan 2-9). The expected CSV output path is /home/ga/Documents/inactivity_report_2013.csv. All metadata values are consistent with implementation code and verified through cross-reference of task.json, setup scripts, export scripts, and verifier logic.
